title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has responded to a 2020 report on artificial intelligence and public standards, outlining its approach to addressing issues raised in the report. The government aims to balance the benefits and risks of artificial intelligence while upholding public standards.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: The report examines the effects of using copyrighted materials in the creation of AI systems, and its findings may have significant implications for the development and use of AI technology. The assessment likely explores the balance between innovation and intellectual property rights in the context of AI.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The UK Department for Education has issued a policy on the use of generative artificial intelligence in education. The policy outlines the department's position on how generative AI should be used in teaching and learning.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This document outlines the UK government's plan for safely using artificial intelligence (AI) in transport. It aims to harness the benefits of AI while minimizing possible drawbacks in the transportation sector.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This report explores the use of copyrighted materials in the development of AI systems and presents updates on progress made under the Data (Use and Access) Act. It aims to provide insights into the balance between copyright protection and AI system development.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government is responding to a report that recommended regulations for artificial intelligence used as medical devices. This response outlines how the government will implement these regulations to ensure safe and effective use of AI in medical settings.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: Ofqual, a UK qualifications regulator, is developing an approach to oversee the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector. This policy aims to ensure that AI is used responsibly and does not compromise the integrity of qualifications.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has responded to a report highlighting concerns about artificial intelligence. The response outlines how the government plans to address and improve regulations around AI in the UK.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference and frequently asked questions for a committee reviewing how artificial intelligence aligns with public standards. The committee will investigate the use of AI and its implications for society, focusing on its relationship with public values and ethics.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Council for Science and Public Health (CSPL) has submitted evidence to a UK government inquiry on how to regulate Artificial Intelligence (AI). The submission aims to inform the development of governance policies for AI.
